examples.sentence: 彼の部屋はゴミだらけだ。
examples.translation: His room is full of trash.
examples.translationEn: His room is full of trash.
examples.romaji: Kare no heya wa gomi darake da.

examples.sentence: このビーチは岩だらけで泳ぎにくい。
examples.translation: This beach is full of rocks and is difficult to swim at.
examples.translationEn: This beach is full of rocks and is difficult to swim at.
examples.romaji: Kono bīchi wa iwa darake de oyogi nikui.

examples.sentence: 彼の顔はしわだらけだ。
examples.translation: His face is covered with wrinkles.
examples.translationEn: His face is covered with wrinkles.
examples.romaji: Kare no kao wa shiwa darake da.

examples.sentence: このリンゴは虫食いだらけだ。
examples.translation: This apple is riddled with wormholes.
examples.translationEn: This apple is riddled with wormholes.
examples.romaji: Kono ringo wa mushikui darake da.

examples.sentence: こんなに雪が降っているんだから、学校に間に合いっこない。
examples.translation: With snow falling this heavily, there's no way we'll make it to school on time.
examples.translationEn: With snow falling this heavily, there's no way we'll make it to school on time.
examples.romaji: Konna ni yuki ga futteirun dakara, gakkō ni maniai kkonai.

examples.sentence: 彼は泳げないので、プールの向こう側に泳いで行きっこない。
examples.translation: Since he can’t swim, there’s no way he’ll make it to the other side of the pool.
examples.translationEn: Since he can’t swim, there’s no way he’ll make it to the other side of the pool.
examples.romaji: Kare wa oyogenai node, pūru no mukōgawa ni oyoide ikkonai.

examples.sentence: 彼女はとても怖がりなので、一人で夜の公園に行きっこない。
examples.translation: She’s so timid, there’s no chance she’d go to the park alone at night.
examples.translationEn: She’s so timid, there’s no chance she’d go to the park alone at night.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa totemo kowagari na node, hitori de yoru no kōen ni ikkonai.

examples.sentence: この短い時間で、その本を全部読むことはできっこない。
examples.translation: It's impossible to read the entire book in such a short time.
examples.translationEn: It's impossible to read the entire book in such a short time.
examples.romaji: Kono mijikai jikan de, sono hon o zenbu yomu koto wa dekikkonai.

examples.sentence: 彼はテレビを見つつ、宿題をしていました。
examples.translation: He was doing his homework while watching TV.
examples.translationEn: He was doing his homework while watching TV.
examples.romaji: Kare wa terebi o mitsutsu, shukudai o shite imashita.

examples.sentence: 最近忙しいと思いつつ、毎日運動を続けています。
examples.translation: Although I feel busy these days, I still exercise every day.
examples.translationEn: Although I feel busy these days, I still exercise every day.
examples.romaji: Saikin isogashii to omoi tsutsu, mainichi undō o tsuzukete imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は泣きつつ、笑顔で別れを告げた。
examples.translation: She was crying, yet she said goodbye with a smile on her face.
examples.translationEn: She was crying, yet she said goodbye with a smile on her face.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa naki tsutsu, egao de wakare o tsugeta.

examples.sentence: 雨に濡れつつ、彼は歩き続けました。
examples.translation: He continued walking even though he was getting soaked by the rain.
examples.translationEn: He continued walking even though he was getting soaked by the rain.
examples.romaji: Ame ni nure tsutsu, kare wa aruki tsuzukemashita.

examples.sentence: 地球温暖化は進行しつつある。
examples.translation: Global warming is in progress.
examples.translationEn: Global warming is in progress.
examples.romaji: Chikyū ondanka wa shinkō shitsutsu aru.

examples.sentence: この町は急速に発展しつつある。
examples.translation: This town is rapidly developing.
examples.translationEn: This town is rapidly developing.
examples.romaji: Kono machi wa kyūsoku ni hatten shitsutsu aru.

examples.sentence: 子供たちは成長しつつある。
examples.translation: The children are growing up.
examples.translationEn: The children are growing up.
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi wa seichō shitsutsu aru.

examples.sentence: 景気が回復しつつある。
examples.translation: The economy is recovering.
examples.translationEn: The economy is recovering.
examples.romaji: Keiki ga kaifuku shitsutsu aru.

examples.sentence: 窓を開けっぱなしにして、部屋が寒くなってしまった。
examples.translation: I left the window open, and the room got cold.
examples.translationEn: I left the window open, and the room got cold.
examples.romaji: Mado o akeppanashi ni shite, heya ga samuku natte shimatta.

examples.sentence: 彼は音楽を聴くのが好きで、いつもイヤホンをつけっぱなしです。
examples.translation: He loves listening to music and always has his earphones on.
examples.translationEn: He loves listening to music and always has his earphones on.
examples.romaji: Kare wa ongaku o kiku no ga suki de, itsumo iyahon o tsukeppanashi desu.

examples.sentence: 子供はおもちゃを床に置きっぱなしで、片付けなかった。
examples.translation: The child left the toys on the floor and didn’t put them away.
examples.translationEn: The child left the toys on the floor and didn’t put them away.
examples.romaji: Kodomo wa omocha o yuka ni okippanashi de, katazukenakatta.

examples.sentence: 昨夜は忙しかったので、テレビをつけっぱなしにして寝てしまった。
examples.translation: I was busy last night, so I left the TV on and fell asleep.
examples.translationEn: I was busy last night, so I left the TV on and fell asleep.
examples.romaji: Sakuya wa isogashikatta node, terebi o tsukeppanashi ni shite nete shimatta.

examples.sentence: 彼女は大人っぽい服を着ている。
examples.translation: She’s wearing clothes that look mature.
examples.translationEn: She’s wearing clothes that look mature.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa otonappoi fuku o kite iru.

examples.sentence: この説明は分かりにくっぽい。
examples.translation: This explanation seems hard to understand.
examples.translationEn: This explanation seems hard to understand.
examples.romaji: Kono setsumei wa wakari nikuppoi.

examples.sentence: 彼は疲れたっぽい顔をしている。
examples.translation: He has a face that looks tired.
examples.translationEn: He has a face that looks tired.
examples.romaji: Kare wa tsukareta ppoi kao o shite iru.

examples.sentence: この部屋は寒っぽい感じがする。
examples.translation: This room feels kind of chilly.
examples.translationEn: This room feels kind of chilly.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa samuppoi kanji ga suru.

examples.sentence: こんなに忙しいのに、無理に遊んでいられない。
examples.translation: I'm so busy that I can't afford to just play around.
examples.translationEn: I'm so busy that I can't afford to just play around.
examples.romaji: Konna ni isogashii noni, muri ni asonde irarenai.

examples.sentence: 寒すぎて、外で待っていられない。
examples.translation: It's too cold; I can't keep waiting outside.
examples.translationEn: It's too cold; I can't keep waiting outside.
examples.romaji: Samusugite, soto de matte irarenai.

examples.sentence: この問題は重要だから、放っておいていられない。
examples.translation: This issue is too important to ignore.
examples.translationEn: This issue is too important to ignore.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa jūyō dakara, houtte oite irarenai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の態度が我慢できず、黙っていられない。
examples.translation: I can't stand her attitude, so I can't stay silent.
examples.translationEn: I can't stand her attitude, so I can't stay silent.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no taido ga gaman dekizu, damatte irarenai.

examples.sentence: こんなに暑くてかなわない。
examples.translation: I can’t stand this heat.
examples.translationEn: I can’t stand this heat.
examples.romaji: Konnani atsukute kanawanai.

examples.sentence: 彼の話は失礼すぎてかなわない。
examples.translation: His remarks are so rude I can’t put up with them.
examples.translationEn: His remarks are so rude I can’t put up with them.
examples.romaji: Kare no hanashi wa shitsurei sugite kanawanai.

examples.sentence: 映画があまりに感動的で、忘れられなくてかなわない。
examples.translation: The movie was so moving that I can’t stand how it stays on my mind.
examples.translationEn: The movie was so moving that I can’t stand how it stays on my mind.
examples.romaji: Eiga ga amari ni kandō-teki de, wasurerarenakute kanawanai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の笑顔がまぶしくてかなわない。
examples.translation: Her smile is so dazzling that it’s overwhelming.
examples.translationEn: Her smile is so dazzling that it’s overwhelming.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no egao ga mabushikute kanawanai.
